title: Reflection Accounting Bug: _takeCharity Mints Tokens Without Adjusting _rTotal, Causing 10,969x Balance Inflation
claim: The `_takeCharity` function adds `rCharity = tCharity * currentRate` to `_rOwned[address(this)]` but does NOT reduce `_rTotal` by the same amount. The `_rTotal` is only reduced by the 1% reflect fee (`_reflectFee` reduces `_rTotal` by `rFee`). Since the 8% charity fee is taken from the sender (deducted from their `_rOwned`) but credited to the contract without reducing `_rTotal`, the invariant `sum(_rOwned) == _rTotal` is broken. Each transfer creates a net increase in total r-space of `rCharity - rFee = (8% - 1%) * tAmount * currentRate`. Over time, `_rTotal` becomes drastically smaller than the sum of all `_rOwned` balances, causing `_getRate()` to return an artificially low value and `tokenFromReflection()`/`balanceOf()` to return massively inflated balances.
impact: On-chain evidence confirms the pair's SHOCO balance is 10,969x the total supply (1.096e28 vs 1e24). The Uniswap pair uses these inflated balances for constant-product pricing, making SHOCO tokens effectively worthless in economic terms â€” buyers receive 10,969x more tokens than fair value, and sellers receive 10,969x less WETH. The token contract itself holds 9,669x the total supply in its balance. The ERC20 invariant `sum(balanceOf) == totalSupply()` is completely broken. Any value held in the SHOCO-WETH pool is extractable via the auto-swap mechanism (see F-002).
paths: Any transfer where fees are active (trading open, sender/recipient not excluded from fees) triggers the bug: `_transfer` â†’ `_tokenTransfer` â†’ `_transferStandard` (or variant) â†’ `_takeCharity(tCharity)` adds `rCharity` to `_rOwned[address(this)]` while `_reflectFee(rFee, tFee)` only reduces `_rTotal` by `rFee`. The charity portion (8% vs 1% reflect) is effectively minted into circulation.

Exploit path (per FlawVerifier): (1) Flash-loan WETH from any liquid pair, (2) Buy SHOCO via Uniswap â€” the inflated reserves give the attacker ~10,969x more SHOCO than fair value, (3) Sell a portion of SHOCO to trigger auto-swap which dumps the contract's accumulated balance, (4) Repay flash loan, keep remaining SHOCO. Optimal flash loan: 8e15 WETH with buy divisor 32 (buy ~2.5e14 WETH worth).

title: Auto-Swap Can Be Manipulated to Dump Inflated Contract Balance, Draining Pair Liquidity
claim: The auto-swap mechanism in `_transfer` triggers whenever the token contract's balance exceeds `_numOfTokensToExchangeForTeamDev` (5e15) and the sender is not the Uniswap pair. Due to F-001, the contract's balance is massively inflated (9.67e27 on-chain, ~1.9 million times the threshold). On ANY non-pair transfer (e.g., a sell), `swapTokensForEth(contractTokenBalance)` sells ALL contract tokens through Uniswap with `amountOutMin=0` â€” dumping an enormous quantity of tokens that drains WETH from the pool. An attacker can trigger this atomically within a flash-loan-backed trade to extract value.
impact: The auto-swap sells the entire inflated contract balance (9.67e27 SHOCO) for WETH. With the pool holding only ~0.00041 WETH, the auto-swap extracts nearly all of it. Half the WETH goes to the team dev address (0x7109...), half remains stuck in the contract. The massive sell causes extreme price impact, making subsequent sells within the same transaction receive almost no WETH. Combined with F-001, an attacker can: (a) buy SHOCO at the inflated cheap price, (b) sell a small amount to trigger the auto-swap, (c) the auto-swap extracts the pool's WETH, (d) the attacker's buy-side profit comes from receiving SHOCO at 10,969x below fair value.
paths: Trigger: Any `transfer` or `transferFrom` where sender != uniswapV2Pair and contract balance >= 5e15 (always true due to F-001). The auto-swap fires BEFORE the actual token transfer.

Flash-loan attack: (1) Borrow WETH from liquid pool, (2) Call swapExactTokensForTokensSupportingFeeOnTransferTokens to buy SHOCO with ~2.5e14 WETH â†’ receives ~2.7e27 SHOCO due to inflation, (3) Sell a portion of SHOCO via swapExactTokensForTokensSupportingFeeOnTransferTokens â†’ `_transfer` triggers auto-swap â†’ contract dumps 9.67e27 SHOCO â†’ drains ~2e14 WETH from pool â†’ attacker's sell gets remaining WETH, (4) Repay flash loan (repayAmount = loan * 1000/997 + 1), keep leftover SHOCO as profit.

title: Half of Auto-Swap ETH Permanently Locked in Contract with Renounced Ownership
claim: `sendETHToTeamDev(uint256 amount)` sends only `amount.div(2)` (half) of the contract's ETH balance to the team wallet. The other half remains in the contract. The only way to trigger `sendETHToTeamDev` is via the auto-swap path in `_transfer` (which also sends only half) or via `manualSend()` which is `onlyOwner`. Since `owner()` returns `address(0)` (ownership renounced), `manualSend()` can never be called again. Any ETH in the contract from past auto-swaps is permanently locked.
impact: ETH accumulated from past auto-swaps is permanently inaccessible. Future auto-swaps will continue sending only half of the new ETH to the team, leaving the other half locked. This represents a continuous loss of value for the team/dev wallet.
paths: Every auto-swap: `_transfer` â†’ `swapTokensForEth(contractBalance)` â†’ receives ETH â†’ `sendETHToTeamDev(address(this).balance)` â†’ only `balance/2` sent to team, rest stays. `manualSend()` is gated by `onlyOwner` with owner = address(0), permanently inaccessible.

title: Cooldown Can Be Bypassed Using Multiple Proxy/Contract Wallets
claim: The 60-second cooldown is tracked per-address in `_lastTx[sender]`. An attacker can deploy multiple contract wallets and rotate between them to bypass the cooldown restriction. Each new contract address has `_lastTx` initialized to 0, so the first transaction always passes. Combined with the `uniswapOnly` flag, direct wallet-to-wallet transfers are blocked, but the cooldown still applies to Uniswap trades when `cooldownEnabled=true` and `uniswapOnly=true`.
impact: The cooldown mechanism provides only a minor friction for determined attackers. Since `uniswapOnly=true` prevents direct transfers anyway, this is primarily relevant if `_removeDestLimit()` is ever called to set `uniswapOnly=false`.
paths: Deploy N contract wallets. Rotate through them: each buys/sells via Uniswap once per 60s window. Since cooldown is per-sender-address, a new address has no cooldown.
